8 floors will require 80 man-days to clean.

Problem 8.4 Three quantities A, B, C are such that AB =
KC, where k is a constant. When A is kept constant, B varies directly as C; when B is kept constant, A varies directly
C and when C is kept constant, A varies inversely as B.
Initially, A was at 5 and A : B : C was 1 : 3 : 5. Find the
value of A when B equals 9 at constant C.
(a) 8
(b) 8.33
(c) 9
(d) 9.5
Solution Initial values are 5, 15 and 25.
Thus we have 5 × 15 = K × 25.
Hence, K = 3.
Thus, the equation is AB = 3C.
For the problem, keep C constant at 25. Then, A × 9 = 3 ×
25.
i.e., A = 75/9 = 8.33

Problem 8.7 The ratio of water and milk in a 30 litre
mixture is 7 : 3. Find the quantity of water to be added to
the mixture in order to make this ratio 6 : 1.
(a) 30
(b) 32
(c) 33
(d) 35
Solution Solve while reading Æ As you read the →rst
sentence, you should have 21 litres of water and 9 litres
of milk in your mind.
In order to get the →nal result, we keep the milk constant
at 9 litres.
Then, we have 9 litres, which corresponds to 1
Hence, ‘?’ corresponds to 6.
Solving by using unitary method we have
54 litres of water to 9 litres of milk.
Hence, we need to add 33 litres of water to the original
mixture.
Alternatively, we can solve this by using options. The
student should try to do the same.
Problem 8.8 Three containers A, B and C are having
mixtures of milk and water in the ratio of 1 : 5, 3 : 5 and
5 : 7, respectively. If the capacities of the containers are
in the ratio 5 : 4 : 5, →nd the ratio of milk to water, if the
mixtures of all the three containers are mixed together.
Solution Assume that there are 500, 400 and 500 litres
respectively in the 3 containers.
Then we have, 83.33,150 and 208.33 litres of milk in
each of the three containers.
Thus, the total milk is 441.66 litres. Hence, the amount of
water in the mixture is
1400 – 441.66 = 958.33 litres.
Hence, the ratio of milk to water is
441.66 : 958.33 Æ 53 : 115 (Using division by 0.33333)
The calculation thought process should be:
(441 × 3 + 2) : (958 × 3 + 1) = 1325 : 2875.
Dividing by 25 Æ 53 : 115.

questions.text:
If 10 tractors can plough 1/6th of a →eld in 12 days,
how many days 20 tractors will take to do the remaining work?
questions.solution: 120 tractors days = 1/6 of the field Æ 720 tractors days are required to plough the field. Thus, the 5 remaining work would be 720 ¥ = 600 tractors 6 days. With 20 tractors, it would take 30 days.

questions.text:
A cow takes 5 leaps for every 4 leaps of a goat, but
3 leaps of the goat are equal to 4 leaps of the cow.
What is the ratio of the speed of the cow to that of
the goat?
questions.solution: Assume that 1 cow leap is equal to 3 metres and 1 goat leap is equal to 4 metres. Then the speed of the cow in one unit time = 3 ¥ 5 = 15 meters. Also, the speed of the goat in one unit time = 4 ¥ 4 = 16 meters. The required ratio is 15:16.

questions.text:
The present ratio of ages of P and Q is 3: 5. 10 years
ago, this ratio was 1: 2. Find the sum total of their
present ages.
questions.solution: 3x, and 5x are their current ages. According to the problem, 3x – 10 : 5x – 10 = 1:2 Æ x = 10 and hence the sum total of their present ages is 80 years (30 + 50 = 80).

Three men rent a farm for `14000 per annum. A puts
220 cows in the farm for 3 months, B puts 220 cows
for 6 months and C puts 880 cows for 3 months.
What percentage of the total expenditure should C
pay?
questions.solution: The share of the rent is on the basis of the ratio of the number of cow months. A uses 660 cow months (220 ¥ 3), B uses 1320(220 ¥ 6) and C uses 2640 cow months (880 ¥ 3) Hence, the required ratio is: 660:1320:2640 = 1:2:4 4 400 Required percentage = ¥ 100 = = 57.14% 7 7

questions.text:
25 students can do a job in 12 days, but on the
starting day, →ve of them informed that they are not
coming. By what fraction will the number of days
required for doing the whole work get increased?
questions.solution: 25 ¥ 12 = 300 man-days is required for the job. If only 20 students turn up, they would require 15 days to complete the task. The number of days is increasing by 1/4.

questions.text:
A dishonest shopkeeper mixed 1 litre of water for
every 3 litres of petrol and thus made up 36 litres
of petrol. If he now adds 15 litres of petrol to the
mixture, →nd the ratio of petrol and water in the new
mixture.
questions.solution: The initial amount of water is 9 litres and petrol is 27 litres. By adding 15 litres of petrol the mixture becomes 42 petrol and 9 water Æ 14:3 the required ratio.

`3000 is distributed among p, q and r such that p
gets 2/3rd of what q and r together get and r gets ½
of what p and q together get. Find r’s share.
questions.solution: From the first statement p = 1200 and q + r = 1800. From the second statement r = 1000 and p + q = 2000.

of gas is inversely proportional to the volume. If
the pressure is reduced by 20%, →nd the respective
change in volume.
questions.solution: Since pressure and volume are inversely proportional, we get that if one is reduced by 20% the other would grow by 25%. Option (b) is correct.

If `232 is divided among 150 children such that each
girl and each boy gets ` 1 and `2 respectively. Then
how many girls are there?
questions.solution: Solve using options. For option (c), 68 girls. Hence, 82 boys Amount with Girls = 68 ¥ 1 = 68 Amount with Boys = 82 ¥ 2 = 164. Total of 68 + 164 = 232. Thus, option (c) fits the conditions.

questions.text:
A beggar had one rupee, two rupees and →ve rupees
coins in the ratio 5: 15: 12 respectively at the end of
day. If that day he earned a total of `95, how many
one rupee coins did he have?
questions.solution: The ratio of the values of the three coins are: 1 ¥ 5 : 15 ¥ 2 : 12 ¥ 5 = 5:30:60 Thus, one rupee coins correspond to `5. Hence, there will be 5 coins.

questions.text:
Sudhir has coins of the denomination of `. 1, 50 p
and 25 p in the ratio of 12 : 10 : 7. The total worth
of the coins he has is `112.5. Find the number of
25 p coins that Sudhir has
questions.solution: Ratio of number of coins = 12:10:7 Ratio of individual values of coins = 1:0.5: 0.25 Ratio of gross value of coins = 12 : 5 : 1.75 = 48 : 20 : 7 Æ 112.5 Thus, he has `10.5 in 25 paisa coins. Which means that he would have 42 such coins.

questions.text:
A cask contains 15 gallons of mixture of wine and
water in the ratio 2 : 1. how much of the water must
be drawn off, so the ratio of wine and water in the
cask may become 4 : 1.
questions.solution: Initially the cask has 10 liters wine and 5 liters of water. If we were to draw out 2.5 litres of water, the ratio of wine to water would become 4:1. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.text:
The total number of pupils in three classes of a
school is 700. The number of pupils in classes I and
II are in the ratio 1 : 4 and those in classes II and
III are in the ratio 3 : 5. Find the number of pupils
in the class that had the highest number of pupils.
questions.solution: The overall ratio is: 3: 12: 20. Dividing 700 in 3: 12: 20, we get number of students in class III (AS 20 Class III has highest number of students) = ¥ 35 700 = 400

questions.text:
Sahil can row a certain course up the stream in 84
minutes; they can row the same course down stream
in 9 minutes less than they can row it in still water.
How long would they take to row down with the
stream.
questions.solution: If you try to solve this question through equations, the process becomes too long and almost inconclusive. The best way to approach this question is by trying to use options. The question asks us to find the time in which the boat can move downstream. The basic situation in this question is: Percentage increase over still water speed while going downstream = Percentage decrease over still water speed while going upstream. (Since: Sdownstream = Sboat + Sstream and Supstream = Sboat – Sstream) Hence, the percentage increase in time while going upstream should match the percentage decrease in time while going downstream in such a way that the percentage change in the speed is same in both the cases). Testing for option (a): Timeupstream = 84 minutes (given) Timedownstream = 45 minutes (first value from the option) TimeStillwater = 54 minutes (45 + 9) % increase in time when going upstream = 30/54 [Note: The percentage increase should be written as 30 ¥ 100/54. However, as I have repeatedly pointed out right from the chapter of percentages, you need to be able to look at % values of ratios directly by using the Percentage rule for calculations) % decrease in time when going downstream = 9/54 = 16.66% Since, the % decrease is 16.66%, this should correspond to a % increase in speed by 20% (Since, product speed ¥ time is constant). This means that the speed should drop by 20% while going upstream and hence the time should increase by 25% while going upstream. But, 30/54 does not give us a value of 25% increase. Hence this option is incorrect. Testing for option (b): Timeupstream = 84 minutes (given) Timedownstream = 63 minutes (first value from the option) TimeStillwater = 72 minutes (63 + 9) % increase in time when going upstream = 12/72 = 16.66% % decrease in time when going downstream = 9/72 = 12.5% Since, the % decrease is 12.5%, this should correspond to a % increase in speed by 14.28% (Since, product speed ¥ time is constant). This means that the speed should drop by 14.28% while going upstream and hence the time should increase by 16.66% while going upstream. This is actually occurring. Hence, this option is correct. Options (c) and (d) can be seen to be incorrect in this context.

The wages of laborers in a factory has increased in
the ratio 22: 25 and their number is decreased in the
ratio 3: 2. What was the original wage bill of the
factory if the present bill is `5000?
questions.solution: Let initial wage = 22x Final wage = 25x Let the initial laborers = 3y Final laborers = 2y Final bill = 25x ¥ 2y = 5000 xy = 100 Hence, the Original wage bill = 22x ¥ 3y = 66 xy = 6600.

salary, the ratio is altered to 4:13. Find their respective salaries.
questions.solution: If their salaries are k, 7k k + 2500 4 = 7 k + 2500 13 On solving, we get k = `1500. Their salaries are `1500, 10500.

questions.solution: 1 : 3 : 5 Æ x, 3x and 5x add up to 18. So the numbers are: 2, 6 and 10. Ratio of cubes = 8 : 216 : 1000 = 1: 27: 125.

questions.text:
In a co-educational school there are 15 more girls
than boys. If the number of girls is increased by 10%
and the number of boys is also increased by 16%,
there would be 9 more girls than boys. What is the
number of students in the school?
questions.solution: Solve using options. For option (c), we will get that initially there are 125 boys and 140 girls. After the given increases, the number of boys would be 145 and the number of girls would become 154 which gives a difference of 9 as required.

questions.text:
At IIM Bangalore class of 1995, Sonali, a →rst year

(worth 4 points each), B (worth 3 points each) and
C (worth 2 points each). Her grade point average
is 3.2, and if the course in which she get C’s were
deleted, her GPA in the remaining courses would be
3.333. How many A’s, B’s and C’s did she get?
questions.solution: From the question, it is evident that after leaving out the C courses, Sonali’s GPA goes to 3.33. This means that the number of subjects she must have had after leaving out the C’s must be a multiple of

questions.text:
Total expenses of running the hostel at Harvard
Business School are partly →xed and partly varying
linearly with the number of boarders. The average
expense per boarder is $70 when there are 25 boarders and $60 when there are 50 boarders. What is the
average expense per boarder when there are 100
boarders?
questions.solution: When there are 25 boarders, the total expenses are $1750. When there are 50 boarders, the total expenses are $ 3000. The change in expense due to the coming in of 25 boarders is $ 1250. Hence, expense per boarder is equal to $50. This also means that when there are 25 boarders, the variable cost would be 25 ¥ 50 = $1250. Hence, $500 must be the fixed expenses. So for 100 boarders, the total cost would be: $ 500 (fixed) + $ 5000 = $5500

questions.text:
The speed of the engine of Gondwana Express is
42 km/h when no compartment is attached, and the
reduction in speed is directly proportional to the
square root of the number of compartments attached.
If the speed of the train carried by this engine is 24
km/h when 9 compartments are attached, the maximum number of compartments that can be carried
by the engine is
 Three drunkards agree to pool their vodka and decided
to share it with a fourth drunkard (who had no vodka)
at a price equal to 5 roubles a litre. The →rst drunkard
contributed 1 litre more than the second and the second
contributed a litre more than the third. Then all four
of them divided the vodka equally and drank it. The
fourth drunkard paid money, which was divided in
the ratio of each drunkard’s contribution towards his
portion. It was found that the →rst drunkard should get
twice as much money as the second. Based on this
information answer the questions 26–28. (Assume that
all shares are integral).
questions.solution: S = 42 – k÷n 24 = 42 – k ¥ 3 Æ k = 6 So, S = 42 – 6÷n For 49 compartments the train would not move. Hence it would move for 48 compartments. 26-28: Let the third drunkard get in x litres. Then the second will contribute x + 1 and the first will contribute x + 2 litres. Thus in all they have 3x + 3 litres of the drink. Using option a in question 27, this value is 12, giving x as 3. Also, each drunkard will drink 3 litres. Thus, the first drunkard brings 5 litres and the second 4 litres. Their contribution to the fourth drunkard will be in the ratio 2:1 and hence their share of money would be also in the ratio 2:1. Hence, this option is correct for question 27. Hence, for question 26, the second drunkard will get 5 roubles (for his contribution of 1 litre to the fourth) and for question 28, the answer would be 1:3

questions.text:

How many litres of vodka was consumed in all by

In Ramnagar Colony, the ratio of school going children to non-school going children is 5 : 4. If in the
next year, the number of non-school going children
is increased by 20%, making it 35,400, what is the
new ratio of school going children to non-school
going children?
questions.solution: 5 : 4 Æ 5 : 4.8 Æ 25 : 24. Option (c) is correct.

questions.text:
A precious stone weighing 35 grams worth ` 12,250
is accidentally dropped and gets broken into two
pieces having weights in the ratio of 2 : 5. If the price
varies as the square of the weight then →nd the loss
incurred.
questions.solution: P = K ¥ W2 Æ 12250 = K ¥ 352 Æ K = 10. Thus our price and weight relationship is: P = 10W2. When the two pieces are in the ratio 2:5 (weight wise) then we know that their weights must be 10 grams and 25 grams respectively. Their values would be: 10 gram piece: 10 ¥ 102 = ` 1000; 25 gram piece: 10 ¥ 252 = ` 6250. Total Price = 1000 + 62450 = 7250. From an initial value of 12250, this represents a loss of ` 5000.

questions.text:
On his deathbed, Mr. Kalu called upon his three
sons and told them to distribute all his assets worth
` 5,25,000 in the ratio of 1/15 : 1/21 : 1/35 amongst
themselves. Find the biggest share amongst the three
portions.
questions.solution: The ratio of distribution should be: 21 ¥ 35 : 15 ¥ 35 : 15 ¥ 21 Æ 147 : 105:63 Æ 7:5:3 The biggest share will be worth: 7 ¥ 525000/15 = 245000.

questions.text:
Three jackals—Paar, Maar and Taar together have
675 loaves of bread. Paar has got three times as
much as Maar but 25 loaves more than Taar. How
many does Taar have?

King Sheru had ordered the distribution of apples
according to the following plan : for every 20 apples
the elephant gets, the zebra should get 13 apples
and the deer should get 8 apples. Now his servant
Shambha jackal is in a →x. Can you help him by
telling how much should he give to the elephant if
there were 820 apples in total?
questions.solution: Ratio of distribution = 20 : 13 : 8 So the elephant should get (20/41) ¥ 820 = 400.

questions.text:
In the famous Bhojpur island, there are four men for
every three women and →ve children for every three
men. How many children are there in the island if it
has 531 women?
questions.solution: Women : Men = 3 : 4 Men : Children = 3 : 5 Æ Women : Men : children = 9 : 12 : 20 In the ratio, 9 Æ 531 Women Thus, 20 Æ 1180 children.

questions.text:
Which of the following will have the maximum
change in their values if 5 is added to both the numerator and denominator of all the fractions?
questions.solution: 2/3 becomes 7/8 a change from 0.666 to 0.875 while the other changes are smaller than this. For instance 4/7 becomes 9/12 a change from 0.5714 to 0.75 which is smaller than the change in 2/3. Similarly, the other options can be checked and rejected.

questions.text:
40 men could have →nished the whole project in 28
days but due to the inclusion of a few more men,
work got done in 3/4 of the time. Find out how many
more men were included (in whole numbers).
questions.solution: Since, the work gets done in 25% less time there must have been an addition of 33.33% men. This would mean 13.33 men extra Æ which would mean 14 extra men (in whole numbers)

questions.text:
Mr AM, the magnanimous cashier at XYZ Ltd., while

realises that the salaries of A, B and C are now in
the ratio 4 : 5 : 7. The salary of C could be
questions.solution: From the given options, we just need to look for a multiple of 7. 2100 is the only option which is a multiple of 7 and is hence the correct answer.

questions.text:

the interest rate is 20% compounded annually and
if each received the same amount as the other when
she attained the age of 20 years, their shares are
Directions for questions 39 to 41: Read the passage
below and answer the questions that follow:
Anshu gave Bobby and Chandana as many pens as each
one of them already had. Then Chandana gave Anshu and
Bobby as many pens as each already had. Now each had an
equal number of pens.The total number of pens is 72.
questions.solution: This is a simple question if you can catch hold of the logic of the question, i.e., the younger daughter’s share must be such after adding a CI of 20% for two years, she should get the same value as her elder sister. None of the options meets this requirement. Hence, None of these is correct. 39-41. You should realise that when Anshu gives her pens to Bobby & Chandana, the number of pens for both Bobby & Chandana should double. Also, the number of pens for Anshu & Bobby should also double when Chandana gives off her pens. Further the final condition is that each of them has 24 pens. The following table will emerge on the basis of this logic. Anshu Bobby Chandana Final 24 24 24 Second round 12 12 48 Initial 42 6 24

questions.text: How many pens did Bobby have initially?
How many pens did Chandana have initially?
How many pens did Anshu have initially?

The volume of a pyramid varies jointly as its height
and the area of its base; and when the area of the base
is 60 square dm and the height 14 dm, the volume
is 280 cubic dm. What is the area of the base of a
pyramid whose volume is 390 cubic dm and whose
height is 26 dm?
Ratio, Proportion and Variation III.101
questions.solution: V = k AH Æ 280 = k ¥ 60 ¥ 14 Æ 280 = 840k. Thus, k = 1/3 and the equation becomes: V = AH/3 and 390 = 26A/3 Æ A = 45.

questions.text:
The expenses of an all boys’ institute are partly
constant and partly vary as the number of boys. The
expenses were ` 10,000 for 150 boys and ` 8400
for 120 boys. What will the expenses be when there
are 330 boys?
questions.solution: Expenses for 120 boys = 8400 Expenses for 150 boys = 10000. Thus, variable expenses are ` 1600 for 30 boys. If we add 180 more boys to make it 330 boys, III.112 How to Prepare for Quantitative Aptitude for CAT we will get an additional expense of 1600 ¥ 6 = ` 9600. Total expenses are ` 19600.

questions.text:
The distance of the horizon at sea varies as the square
root of the height of the eye above sea-level. When
the distance is 14.4 km, the height of the eye is 18
metres. Find, in kilometres, the distance when the
height of the eye is 8 metres.
questions.solution: Let the distance be d. Then, d/14.4= 8 18 Æ d = 9.6

questions.text:
There are two quantities of oil, with the masses differing by 2 kg. The same quantity of heat, equal to
96 kcal, was imparted to each mass, and the larger

questions.solution: T = KD/V. Also V = (K1 Q )/N where K and K1 are constants, T is the time duration of the journey, Q is the quantity of coal used and N is the number of carriages. Thus, T = (KDN)/(K1 Q ) or T = (K2DN)/( Q ) Æ if we take K/K1 as K2. From the information provided in the question: 30 = (K2¥ 50 ¥ 18)/10 Æ K2 = 1/3 Thus, the equation becomes: T = (DN)/(3 Q ). Then, when D = 42, T = 28, and N = 16 we get: 28 = 42 ¥ 16/(3 Q ) ÆQ = 64

questions.text:
There are two alloys of gold and silver. In the →rst

How many times more must we take of the second
alloy than the →rst in order to obtain a new alloy in
which there would be twice as much silver as gold?
questions.solution: This only occurs in Option c. Hence, that is the answer.

questions.text:
Calculate the weight and the percentage of zinc in
the zinc–copper alloy being given that the latter’s
alloy with 3 kg of pure zinc contains 90 per cent of
zinc and with 2 kg of 90% zinc alloy contains 84%
of zinc.
questions.solution: Suppose you take p = 3 and q = 2. It can be clearly seen that the square root of 2 does not lie between 2 and 3. Hence, option (c) is incorrect. Further with these values for p and q option (a) also can be ruled out since it means that the value should lie between 5 and 6 which it obviously does not. Also, Option (d) gives 6/5 and 1/6. This means that the value should lie between 0.1666 and 1.2 (which it obviously does not). Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.text:
Two solutions, the →rst of which contains 0.8 kg and

mixture if the →rst solution is known to contain 10
per cent more of salt than the second.
questions.solution: Since there is a 12.5% increase in the wages, the cost of digging the pit would get increase by 12.5% - due to the increase in wages. However, since now the workers are working for 5% longer time, you can divide the increased wages by 1.05 to get the required answer. Thus, the answer would be: New 1.125 cost = 2694 ¥ = 2886 (approximately) 1.05

questions.text:
From a full barrel containing 729 litres of honey we
pour off ‘a’ litre and add water to →ll up the barrel.
After stirring the solution thoroughly, we pour off
‘a’ litre of the solution and again add water to →ll up
the barrel. After the procedure is repeated 6 times,
the solution in the barrel contains 64 litres of honey.

questions.solution: The constraint given to us for the values of p, q, and r is r ¥ (p + q) = pq So, if we take p = 6, q = 3 and r = 2, we have 18 = 18 and a feasible set of values for p, q and r respectively. With this set of values, we can complete the operation as defined and see what happens. Wine left in the vessel A = 4 = (6 – 2) Wine in the vessel B = 2 With these values none of the first 3 options matches. Thus, option (d) is correct.

questions.text:
In two alloys, the ratios of nickel to tin are 5 : 2
and 3 : 4 (by weight). How many kilogram of the

together to obtain 28 kg of a new alloy with equal
contents of nickel and tin?

options.a: 9 kg of the →rst alloy and 22 kg of the second
options.b: 17 kg of the →rst alloy and 11 kg of the second
options.c: 7 kg of the →rst alloy and 21 kg of the second

questions.solution: The only information available here is that – b/c should be equal to 9/1. This is not sufficient to make any of the first three options as conclusions. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.text:
In two alloys, aluminium and iron are in the ratios
of 4 : 1 and 1 : 3. After alloying together 10 kg of the
→rst alloy, 16 kg of the second and several kilograms
of pure aluminium, an alloy was obtained in which
the ratio of aluminium to iron was 3 : 2. Find the
weight of the new alloy.
questions.solution: Let the incomes be 4x, 5x, 6x And the spending be 6y, 7y, 8y And savings are (4x – 6y), (5x – 7y) & (6x – 8y) Rahul saves 1/4th of his income. Therefore 4x – 6y = 4x/4 4x – 6y = x 3x = 6y x/y = 2 Therefore y = x/2 Ratio of Rahul’s Saurav’s & Sachin’s savings = 4x – 6y : 5x – 7y : 6x – 8y = x: 5x – 7y: 6x – 8y = x: 5x – 7x/2: 6x – 8x/2 = x: 3x/2: 2x = 2:3:4

questions.text:
There are two alloys of gold, silver and platinum.
The →rst alloy is known to contain 40 per cent of
platinum and the second alloy 26 per cent of silver.
The percentage of gold is the same in both alloys.
Having alloyed 150 kg of the →rst alloy and 250 kg
of the second, we get a new alloy that contains 30
per cent of gold. How many kilogram of platinum
is there in the new alloy?
questions.solution: Solve by taking values of a, b, c, d and e, f, g, and h independently of each other a = 1, b = 2, c = 3, d = 6 and e = 3, f = 9, g = 4 and h = 12 gives (ae + bf) : (ae – bf) = 21: – 15 = – 7/5 Option (b) (cg + dh)/(cg – dh) = 84/ – 60 = – 7/5. This would be the answer, since no other option gives us the same value.

questions.text:
Two alloys of iron have different percentage of iron
in them. The →rst one weighs 6 kg and second one
weighs 12 kg. One piece each of equal weight was

of iron became the same in the resulting two new
alloys. What was the weight of each cut-off piece?

questions.solution: Speed = 36 – k N . Putting value of N = 4 we get: 24 = 36 – 2k. Hence, k = 6. Thus the equation is: S = 36 – 6 N This means that when N = 36, the speed will become zero. Hence, the train can just move when 35 wagons are attached.

questions.text:
Three vessels having volumes in the ratio of 1 : 2 : 3
are full of a mixture of coke and soda. In the →rst
vessel, ratio of coke and soda is 2 : 3, in second, 3 : 7

and in third, 1 : 4. If the liquid in all the three vessels were mixed in a bigger container, what is the
resulting ratio of coke and soda?

questions.text:

produce the →rst quality and if they are mixed in the
ratio of 2 : 3, the second quality is obtained. How
many kilograms of the →rst quality has to be mixed
with 10 kg of the second quality so that a third quality having the two varieties in the ratio of 7 : 11 may
be produced?
questions.solution: Let x=5 Then y = f(x) = 6/4 = 1.5 And f(y) = 2.5/0.5 = 5. Thus, the ratio of x : f(y) = 1 : 1 Note: Even if you take some other value of y, you would still get the same answer.

questions.text:
A toy weighing 24 grams of an alloy of two metals

price of the other metal in the alloy used to make

questions.solution: In 2000 man weeks before the calamity, 5/6th of the work is completed. Hence, 2400 men weeks will be the total amount of work. However, due to the calamity half the work gets washed off Æ This means that 1000 man weeks worth of work must have got washed off. This leaves 1400 men weeks of work to be completed by the 140 men. They will take 10 more weeks and hence the total time required is 24 weeks.

questions.solution: Total distances covered under each mode = 32, 4 and 12 km respectively. Total charges = 32 ¥ 24 + 4 ¥ 3 + 12 ¥ 12 = `924.

questions.text:
There are two qualities of milk—Amul and Sudha
having different prices per litre, their volumes being
